title: [DSE] Introduce `eliminateRedundantStoresViaDominatingConditions`
author: antoniofrighetto
description:
While optimizing tautological assignments, if there exists a dominating condition that implies the value being stored in a pointer, and such a condition appears in a node that dominates the store via equality edge, then subsequent stores may be redundant, if no write occurs in between. This is achieved via a DFS top-down walk of the dom-tree, collecting dominating conditions and propagating them to each subtree, popping them upon backtracking.

This also generalizes `dominatingConditionImpliesValue` transform, which was previously taking into account only the immediate dominator.

Compile-time: https://llvm-compile-time-tracker.com/compare.php?from=f8906704104e446a7482aeca32d058b91867e05c&to=24c5d61f1e28acbe6a59ea4e9a5da0ffcee3bf1a&stat=instructions:u.

Compile-time w/ limit on recursion: https://llvm-compile-time-tracker.com/compare.php?from=24c5d61f1e28acbe6a59ea4e9a5da0ffcee3bf1a&to=9889567fe8a0515ab895b22003c93fabfd9ac4e5&stat=instructions:u. Seems to alleviate the small regression in stage2-O3, but seemingly adds one in stage2-O0-g.

tests.test_body:
define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition_clobber_between(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.eq, label %end

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %entry
  br label %next

next:                                             ; preds = %if.eq
  call void @opaque(ptr %x)
  br i1 %c, label %inner, label %end

inner:                                            ; preds = %next
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %next, %entry
  ret void
}

declare void @opaque(ptr)

define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition_3(ptr %x, ptr %y, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %val.2 = load i32, ptr %y, align 4
  %cmp.2 = icmp eq i32 %val.2, 0
  br i1 %cmp.2, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  store i32 0, ptr %y, align 4
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %join
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %entry
  ret void
}

define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %join
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %entry
  ret void
}

define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition_volatile_store(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  store volatile i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %join, %entry
  ret void
}

define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition_atomic_store(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  store atomic i32 0, ptr %x release, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %join, %entry
  ret void
}

define void @remove_tautological_store_via_dom_condition_2(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %join
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %entry
  ret void
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--PREMERGE ADVISOR COMMENT: Linux-->
# :penguin: Linux x64 Test Results

* 191555 tests passed
* 4982 tests skipped

:white_check_mark: The build succeeded and all tests passed.

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> Otherwise you would probably want to walk the dom-tree and remember which relevant dominating conditions you currently know about.

I wonder if it may be worthwhile generalizing the approach in this PR to have instead a `DSEState::eliminateRedundantStoresUsingDomConditions()` to walk the dom-tree top-down, push `*Ptr == Val` facts (for children dominated by the node), and iterate over store later checking for redundancy, instead of the current approach of walking the dom-tree per-store.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> > Otherwise you would probably want to walk the dom-tree and remember which relevant dominating conditions you currently know about.
> 
> I wonder if it may be worthwhile generalizing the approach in this PR to have instead a `DSEState::eliminateRedundantStoresUsingDomConditions()` to walk the dom-tree top-down, push `*Ptr == Val` facts (for children dominated by the node), and iterate over store later checking for redundancy, instead of the current approach of walking the dom-tree per-store.

Yes, this is what I had in mind. Doing this walk per-store is expensive.

Thanks for clarifying, PR (and description) updated. I'm not exactly sure where the small regressions in stage2-O0-g may come from, may as well look into that if looks on the correct direction.

comments.body:

>
